title: Virtue Is Sensible Of Suffering.
content:
Though a wise man all pressures can sustain,
    His virtue still is sensible of pain:
    Large shoulders though he has, and well can bear,
    He feels when packs do pinch him, and the where.
meaning:
"Virtue Is Sensible Of Suffering." is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
